headline: The House passes permanent daylight saving time by a wide margin while Senate debates defense spending and judicial nominees!

senate.lastSessionSummary: The Senate continued work on nominations and passed several commemorative measures, including the Older Americans Act Reauthorization Act. The chamber voted 50-46 to reject a cloture motion on the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2027, and cloture votes are scheduled for upcoming judicial nominations.

onTheFloor.title: National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2027
onTheFloor.status: Motion to proceed rejected 50-46; motion to reconsider entered

onTheFloor.title: Jeffrey T. Kuntz to be United States District Judge for the Southern District of Florida
onTheFloor.status: Cloture vote scheduled for Wednesday, July 15 at 12 noon; confirmation vote at 2 p.m. if cloture invoked

onTheFloor.title: Kara Marie Westercamp to be Judge of the United States Court of International Trade
onTheFloor.status: Cloture vote scheduled for Thursday, July 16

onTheFloor.title: Designating June 2026 as Great Outdoors Month
Congratulating the 2025-2026 Carolina Hurricanes for winning the Stanley Cup

house.lastSessionSummary: The House passed three significant bills under floor consideration: the Sunshine Protection Act making daylight saving time permanent (308-117), the Protecting Privacy in Purchases Act on firearms merchant codes (221-201), and passed two other bills under suspension of the rules on penny minting and bank acquisition.

onTheFloor.title: Sunshine Protection Act - Make daylight savings time permanent
Protecting Privacy in Purchases Act - Prohibit payment networks from assigning special merchant codes for firearms retailers
Common Cents Act - Stop minting the penny and round cash transactions to nearest five cents
Failing Bank Acquisition Fairness Act - Restrict concentration limit exceptions for failed bank mergers
National Security, Department of State, and Related Programs Appropriations Act, 2027

committees.topic: Semiannual Monetary Policy Report to Congress, 10 a.m. Wednesday
Confirmation hearing on Todd Blanche nomination for Attorney General, 9 a.m. Wednesday
Business meeting on labor statistics commissioner and National Labor Relations Board nominations, 9:45 a.m. Wednesday
Markup on 10 education bureaucracy reduction bills, 10:15 a.m. Wednesday
Health subcommittee markup on drug safety bills and hearing on FDA biomedical innovation, 10 a.m. Wednesday
Markup on Congressional Records Protection Act and other measures, 10 a.m. Wednesday
